rulings.Case Title: JULIE FERRIOT VS. DENNIS PATRICK NIX et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories, Set One
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, April 22, 2013, Line 2. PLAINTIFF JULIE FERRIOT'S Motion to Compel Further Responses to Form Interrogatories, Set One Pro Tem Judge Mr. Anthony Schoenberg, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: GRANT Defendant's motion to compel as narrowed in his reply brief. Plaintiff shall produce to Defendant no later than April 29, 2013: (1) the CD referred to in the opposition brief containing 362 pgs of correspondence between Plaintiff and the insurance company, repair estimates and photos of Plaintiff's vehicle; (2) a color copy of Plaintiff's driver's license; (3) the insurance policy: (4) the insurance declaration page, and (5) verifications to Plaintiff's supplemental responses to form interrogatories, requests for admission, and requests for production. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to tschoenberg@fbm.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)
rulings.Judge: Judge Pro Tem: Mr. Anthony Schoenberg

rulings.Case Title: IP-LOGIX, INC. VS. THINK2BUILD, LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Joinder By Deft For Order Compelling Further Responses To Document
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, April 22, 2013, Line 3. DEFENDANTS THINK2BUILD, LLC and MICHAEL STRASSER'S Joinder by Defendant for Order Compelling Further Responses to Document Pro Tem Judge Mr. Anthony Schoenberg, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: This is a request for joinder in Defendant and Cross-Complainant Think2Build, LLC's motion to compel. By way of background, this matter was originally two separate lawsuits, one being a "trade libel" case, Case No. CGC 12-522724, and the other being a "contract" case, Case No. CGC 12-522554. The two cases are now consolidated under the "contract" case number. The attorneys who represented Think2Build, LLC and co-Defendant Michael J. Strasser in the "trade libel" action, and who continue to represent those parties in the consolidated action (and who are now co-counsel for Think2Build, LLC with the attorneys who filed the pending motion to compel) have filed a "joinder" in the pending motion to compel. They argue that the joinder is necessary because in response to some of their document requests, Plaintiff referred to documents that Plaintiff produced in response to Think2Build LLC's previous document requests that are the subject of the pending motion to compel. No opposition has been filed to the joinder. Accordingly, the Judge Pro Tem's tentative ruling is to grant the joinder. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to tschoenberg@fbm.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)
rulings.Judge: Judge Pro Tem: Mr. Anthony Schoenberg

rulings.Case Title: ANDRE PATTERSON VS. ANDREA R. MILANO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, April 22, 2013, Line 13, DEFENDANTS CATHOLIC CHARITIES, MARINELLA GONCALVES' MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT Denied. Defendants have failed to provide a proper ground for relief in their notice. (CRC 3.1110(a).) The dismissal of complaints filed in other actions is not a proper ground to strike a prayer for relief in the form of punitive damages. (CCP § 436. See Notice, p. 2:22 - 3:16.) The Court does not consider the other grounds asserted in Defendants' Points and Authorities that are not stated in the Notice. (Points & Auth., p. 10:22 - 11:15.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, April 22, 2013, Line 13, DEFENDANT CATHOLIC CHARITIES, MARINELLA GONCALVES' DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT Overruled. Defendants have failed to provide a proper ground for relief in their notice. (CRC 3.1110(a).) The dismissal of complaints filed in other actions is not a proper ground for relief on a demurrer. rulings.Case Title: LEGALMATCH.COM VS. MICHAEL E. VAN CLEVE
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Quash Summons And Motion To Stay
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, April 22, 2013, Line 14, DEFENDANT MICHAEL VAN CLEVE'S Notice Of Motion To Quash Summons And Motion To Stay The motion to quash is granted. "When a nonresident defendant challenges personal jurisdiction the burden shifts to the plaintiff to demonstrate by a preponderance of the evidence that all necessary jurisdictional criteria are met. [citation] This burden must be met by competent evidence in affidavits and authenticated documentary evidence." Ziller Electronics Lab GmbH v. Sup. Crt. (1988) 206 Cal.App.3d 1222, 1232. Here, plaintiff argues that defendant consented to California jurisdiction by entering into the Attorney Membership Agreement. In other words, plaintiff argues that defendant consented to California jurisdiction. The only competent evidence before the Court demonstrates that defendant did not consent to California jurisdiction. Plaintiff has failed to meet its burden of proof. The alternative relief sought (a motion to stay) is moot. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)
